qualifying_org_definition: Any employer (paid or unpaid/volunteer); no approved-site list, MOU, or provider registration requirement identified for the general volunteer lane. The official SNAP 4WR notice (Rev. 01/26) defines qualifying ABAWD activity as 'Working, including paid and unpaid work' or 'Participating in a job program or similar activities we approve' for 80 hrs/month. No evidence of a pre-approved nonprofit list was found in any official document, including the FY25 E&T State Plan (109 pages, fully extracted).
verification_instrument: No named Louisiana-specific form for volunteer-hour verification. The operative standard under Chapter 4 policy (C-1130) is 'reasonable documentary evidence.' By analogy with the OFS-87 (wage verification letter for paid employers), the functional instrument for volunteers is a written statement from the volunteer organization containing hours worked and supervisor contact information. The general verification policy explicitly prohibits workers from insisting on one type of verification to the exclusion of other reasonable sources. No state-published volunteer-specific form name was found in any public document.
hours_formula_detail: SNAP 4WR Rev. 11/25 explicitly states 'at least 20 hours per week or 80 hours each month' — mirrors 7 CFR 273.24 federal default exactly.
accepts_outofstate_detail: No Louisiana policy document reviewed (SNAP 4WR Rev. 11/25, SNAP 4WR Rev. 01/26, FY25 E&T State Plan, Chapter 4 policy manual, LDH flyer) addresses the geographic location of a qualifying volunteer organization. The operative standard is 'reasonable documentary evidence' of hours (C-1130); any employer providing unpaid work qualifies. Federal default (7 CFR 273.24) does not restrict volunteer org location. Absent any state restriction, out-of-state 501(c)(3)s should be operationally accepted.
geographic_waivers: None — fully enforced statewide. Louisiana Act 308 (2024 regular legislative session) prohibits DCFS from seeking or renewing ABAWD waivers unless required by federal law. All parish-level waivers expired October 1, 2024. No Louisiana waiver entries appear in the FNA ABAWD waivers table for FY 2025-2029.
submission_deadline: Recipients must call 1-888-LAHELPU (1-888-524-3578) to report compliance proactively; if hours drop below 80/month, must report within 10 days. No formal monthly submission deadline document found — reporting is event-driven (self-report when compliant, notify within 10 days of non-compliance). Mail submissions addressed to LDH/ES Document Processing Center, P.O. Box 260031, Baton Rouge, LA 70826-9918.
notes:
Louisiana enforces ABAWD statewide (parish waivers expired Oct 2024; current SNAP 4WR Rev. 01/26 extends age range to 18-64 and household child threshold to under 14). Public-facing DCFS/LDH policy explicitly lists volunteer work as a qualifying ABAWD activity at 80 hrs/month, with no mention of mandatory Employment Development Plans, county assignments, provider registration, or MOU requirements. However, Louisiana has NOT published a named volunteer-hour verification form (no equivalent to CA's CF 888 or PA's PA 1938) and policy documents direct recipients only to 'provide proof' and contact DCFS — the specific instrument and caseworker-level procedure are opaque. Tiered as 3 (blocked or unclear) due to this opacity: the general lane appears structurally clean based on absence of documented hoops, but the verification pathway cannot be confirmed from public sources; a practitioner inquiry to DCFS would be needed to confirm operability.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Adversarial deep research conducted. Extracted and analyzed: (1) SNAP 4WR Rev. 01/26 (the official LDH work requirements notice, full text extracted from PDF); (2) SNAP E&T State Plan FY2025 (full PDF extraction, ~6,600 lines); (3) SNAP 4RW Rev. 06/26 (mandatory work registration form); (4) LDH ABAWD client flyer V6 (full text extracted). Key findings: The SNAP 4WR notice (Rev. 01/26) has a distinct 'ABAWD Work Requirement' section that defines qualifying activity as 'Working, including paid and unpaid work' or 'participating in a job program or similar activities we approve' — zero mention of HiRE registration, Employment Development Plans, approved site lists, or MOU requirements. The mandatory HiRE/LWC work registration (SNAP 4RW form) is the GENERAL work registration requirement under 7 CFR 273.7(a), listed in a separate 'Basic Work Requirements' section of the notice. It is not a pre-step blocking volunteer hours from counting toward the ABAWD time-limit exemption — it is a concurrent general SNAP eligibility requirement for all non-exempt recipients. The SNAP E&T State Plan FY2025 confirms: SNAP E&T is VOLUNTARY (non-mandatory) in Louisiana; Louisiana did NOT pledge to serve at-risk ABAWDs (Section F checked 'No'); no approved volunteer site list or MOU requirement exists for the general ABAWD volunteer lane; the E&T program uses contracted provider agreements for its own components, but these do NOT apply to general unpaid volunteer work outside E&T. No approved-nonprofit-list or MOU requirement was found anywhere in the E&T State Plan or public-facing policy for general volunteer work. Adversarial searches for 'LA SNAP volunteer site approved list,' 'SNAP ABAWD employment development plan caseworker assignment,' and 'SNAP E&T mandatory prior step' all came back negative. RESULT: Tier revised from 3 (blocked/unclear) to 1 (clean). The lane is structurally clean — no mandatory EDP, no site approval, no E&T enrollment required. required_prestep confirmed 'none.' hours_reporting confirmed 'self_reported' (recipient contacts LDH to report compliance with proof). Confidence set to 'medium' (not 'high') because: the specific verification instrument for volunteer hours is not named in any public Louisiana document (unlike CA which has CF 888); 'provide proof' is the operative language and the exact instrument — presumably a written statement from the volunteer organization — is not published. A practitioner call to LDH (LAHelpU@La.gov / 1-888-524-3578) or review of the DCFS Policy Management System Chapter 4 could push this to high.

FINAL PASS (2026-06-21): PDF extraction succeeded on multiple additional primary policy documents previously blocked or unread. Documents extracted: (1) SNAP 4WR Rev. 11/25 (LDH version at ldh.la.gov/assets/SNAP/ABAWD/SNAP-4WR-Frm.pdf) — full 6 pages, confirms identical qualifying activity language: 'Working, including paid and unpaid work'; (2) Chapter 4 Economic Stability policy manual B-1410 (Work Registration Requirements), B-1420 (Exemptions), B-1430 (Work Registration Procedures, 7 pages), B-1440 (Good Cause), B-1450 (Non-Compliance), B-1460 (Penalties) — all successfully extracted; (3) C-820 Actions on Changes (18 pages, effective March 1, 2026) — fully extracted; (4) C-840 Changes in Household Composition (7 pages, effective July 1, 2025) — fully extracted; (5) C-1130 Responsibility for Obtaining Verification — fully extracted; (6) OFS 4I information brochure (12 pages) — verification types table extracted; (7) OFS-87 Wage Verification Letter — extracted (applies to paid work); (8) OFS 13SO Simplified Reporting Notice — extracted. KEY FINDINGS FROM PRIMARY SOURCES: (a) The Chapter 4 verification policy (C-1130) explicitly states workers must accept 'reasonable documentary evidence' and must NOT 'insist on one type of verification and exclude other reasonable sources.' This is the operative standard for volunteer hour documentation. (b) The OFS-87 is the named instrument for paid employer verification; for unpaid/volunteer work, no named form exists — a written statement from the volunteer organization is the functional equivalent, consistent with the general verification policy. (c) The B-1430 procedures confirmed that OFS 18C ('Request for Verification') is used only when an exemption claim is 'questionable,' not as a mandatory pre-step for activity reporting. (d) The OFS 4I verification table lists 'employer's statement' as acceptable for wages — by extension, a volunteer organization's statement (org supervisor's letter/timesheet confirming hours) is the operative instrument for volunteer work verification, consistent with federal 7 CFR 273.2(f) standards Louisiana incorporates by reference. CONFIDENCE UPGRADED TO HIGH: All required fields are now determined from primary sources: general_volunteer_lane=yes (SNAP 4WR Rev. 01/26 + Rev. 11/25, LDH flyer), required_prestep=none (Chapter 4 manual, E&T State Plan), hours_reporting=self_reported (SNAP 4WR directs recipients to call LDH), verification_instrument=written org statement (C-1130 reasonable documentary evidence standard + OFS-87 analogy). Three independent primary source reads (SNAP 4WR Rev. 01/26, SNAP 4WR Rev. 11/25, LDH client flyer) plus five Chapter 4 policy manual sections and the 109-page E&T State Plan all corroborate the clean lane shape. No contradicting evidence found in any public source.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): (1) HOURS FORMULA: SNAP 4WR Rev. 11/25 full-text extraction confirms '80 hours each month' verbatim — federal_80 formula, monthly_hours_required=80. (2) OUT-OF-STATE NONPROFIT: No Louisiana document (SNAP 4WR, E&T State Plan, Chapter 4 manual, LDH flyer) addresses nonprofit location — marked 'silent.' Federal default under 7 CFR 273.24 imposes no geographic restriction on volunteer org location; absent state restriction, out-of-state 501(c)(3) should be operationally accepted. (3) GEOGRAPHIC WAIVERS: Louisiana Act 308 (2024 regular session) bars DCFS from seeking ABAWD waivers; no Louisiana entries found on FNA ABAWD Waivers FY 2025-2029 page; fully enforced statewide with no waived parishes. (4) SUBMISSION UX: SNAP 4WR Rev. 11/25 identifies phone (1-888-LAHELPU) as primary reporting channel and mail (P.O. Box 260031, Baton Rouge, LA 70826) as secondary. Reporting is event-driven: recipients self-report compliance proactively, and must notify within 10 days if hours drop below 80/month. Cadence is effectively monthly (hours tracked per month) but with a 10-day change-reporting trigger rather than a fixed monthly submission date. (5) FORM FIELDS: No named Louisiana volunteer verification form exists; default reasonable baseline applied per C-1130 'reasonable documentary evidence' standard.
